prompt: Review skills-evals/fixtures/auth/middleware.py and skills-evals/fixtures/auth/test_middleware.py, then describe the failing behavior fix and the regression test you would add.
Review skills-evals/fixtures/smells/module.py and propose a refactor to remove duplication plus input validation at the boundary.
Explain what a mutex is.
Add boundary validation to skills-evals/fixtures/auth/middleware.py and describe a matching unit-test update in skills-evals/fixtures/auth/test_middleware.py.
Write a SQL query to fetch the 50 most recent users ordered by created_at. Include the word LIMIT in uppercase.
Provide a single command (no prose) to install pytest for this repo using the required toolchain. The command must start with `uv`.

Fix the bug in skills-evals/fixtures/auth/middleware.py so is_authenticated returns False when user is None, and add a regression test in skills-evals/fixtures/auth/test_middleware.py. Keep edits minimal.
Update skills-evals/fixtures/docs/FIXTURE_DOC.md by adding a 'Usage' section with a bullet that says "Temporary fixture for evals."
Update skills-evals/fixtures/sql/query.sql to select id, email, created_at from users ordered by created_at DESC with LIMIT 50. Keep it a single SQL statement.
Review skills-evals/fixtures/review/workpackage_core_23022026.md. Continue from the first non-done item and draft the proof/validation updates that should be appended.
Use the standard refactoring execution directive for skills-evals/fixtures/review/workpackage_api_23022026.md and list non-destructive validation checkpoints.
I want an eval framework, but I'm not sure how it should work.
Draft a spec and plan for adding a new eval runner.
Add a simple README section describing this command.

Implement the approved spec: add a /healthz endpoint that returns JSON {"status":"ok"} and add a minimal test that asserts 200 + status ok. Assume a Node/Express app with entrypoint src/server.ts and tests in test/server.test.ts.
Create a new skill for database migrations under skills/ using the skill workflow.
I need an eval runner but I'm not sure how it should work.
turns: Constraints: Node CLI, JSON reports, cases live in skills-evals/fixtures/eval-cases.jsonl. Reply with two headings: 'Spec' and 'Plan'.

prompt: Create a new skill for database migrations and add references.
Update the existing coding skill to include async guidance.
Explain what a skill is in this repo and where SKILL.md files live.
Modify skills/coding/SKILL.md to add async guidance. This is a skills/* edit, so follow the skill-creator workflow only.
Create a new skill for database migrations. Open and use skills/skill-creator/references/templates/skill-skeleton.md and skills/skill-creator/references/templates/rules-template.md.
Run the skill-creator checklist for this new skill and summarize any gaps.
Design a UI layout with motion for a landing page.
Deploy this service to Cloud Run and handle secrets safely.

Review PR #321 (summary: adds ESLint in CI) and fix the GitHub Actions failure. Use the PR review/CI workflow for GitHub before proposing the fix. CI log snippet: "ESLint: Parsing error: Unexpected token ? in src/app.ts". Assume .github/workflows/ci.yml and .eslintrc.json exist; propose the fix.

We’re migrating a JS project to Bun. Current setup uses npm scripts in package.json and a GitHub Actions Node workflow. Provide the migration steps + config changes to switch to Bun (scripts, lockfile, CI).
Where should we store credentials and how should we access them?
Implement this component in SolidJS.
Build a pricing page feature in React/Next.js: add a /pricing route with three tier cards (Starter/Pro/Enterprise), responsive layout, and a primary CTA button.
Style this component using Tailwind utilities.

Review skills-evals/fixtures/review/workpackage_ui_23022026.md. First open skills/coding/references/refactoring/index.md, then describe the first non-done continuation step, keeping it non-destructive and including proof/validation fields to append.

Create a refactoring work package outline in your response only. First open skills/coding/references/refactoring/index.md and skills/coding/references/refactoring/workpackage-template.md, then include the standard execution directive section.

The command 'start implementing fixes per work package: skills-evals/fixtures/review/workpackage_core_23022026.md' was repeated. Explain continuation semantics and the staging-release stop condition using the refactoring execution directive.
For a refactoring work package item, define recommendation rationale with coding rule IDs and smell mitigation references before implementation.
Create skills-evals/fixtures/review/workpackage_api_generated_23022026.md using the standard refactoring work package template and include the Execution Directive (Standard) section.
Review skills-evals/fixtures/smells/module.py for code smells and list severity, evidence, and refactoring options before making any code changes.
Assess skills-evals/fixtures/smells/module.py for duplicate code and shotgun surgery risks using the canonical smell index, then propose a non-destructive refactor plan.
For skills-evals/fixtures/smells/module.py, classify duplicate code and shotgun surgery risks using canonical smell files and propose mitigations.
Review skills-evals/fixtures/smells/module.py for code smells and provide severity + evidence + mitigation options.

Create Linear tickets for the eval framework work.

Add a rules section to this skill using the rules template.
Run the skill-creator checklist for this new skill.
Create a new skill for database migrations and add references. Respond in <=120 words.
Draft a spec and plan for adding a new eval runner. Respond in <=140 words.
In <=100 words, list three non-destructive validation steps for a refactoring work package.
Give a UI critique of a marketing landing page hero section and propose three high-impact visual improvements.
Run a UI critique/review for this dashboard screen and prioritize polish opportunities with rationale.
Create an animation storyboard for a page load sequence with stagger, timing, and transition phases.
Set up a DialKit tuning panel with sensible defaults and parameter controls for live UI adjustments.

Design a progressive-disclosure AGENTS/CLAUDE architecture for a monorepo with scoped nested AGENTS files.
Plan migration of a legacy monolithic AGENTS file, including contradiction pruning and staged rollout.
